mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2016-11-15 indicates sustained low-rate regime
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that increases household income and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days through higher corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth rate exceeds 150,000 persons in monthly change
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,043 thousand as of 2016-11-15 shows sustained employment levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in retail sales and housing starts signals accelerating consumer and construction demand that boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations. The mechanism requires both RSAFS and HOUST to rise simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand expansion rather than isolated sector strength.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 387,117 million and HOUST at 523 thousand as of 2016-10-15 and 2016-11-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines below 92.5 signal supply-side weakness that constrains corporate profit margins and output growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.9815 as of 2016-11-15 is near the trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where INDPRO < 92.5, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings guidance is revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% as of 2016-11-15 indicates elevated unemployment relative to natural rate
regime_hint: labor market weakness or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (below 1.0%) combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPI above 2.5% year-over-year) signals monetary policy lag that creates financial instability and asset repricing risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 224.804 as of 2016-11-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation pressures
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 48% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at levels above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,441.9 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-07-15
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline by more than 10% month-over-month signal a sharp contraction in residential construction demand that precedes broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings are repriced.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month percentage change falls below -10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 523 thousand units as of 2016-11-15
regime_hint: demand shock or housing market contraction
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
